title: Welcome to Your Knowledge Base
content:
## Getting Started

This is your personal knowledge base. Every piece of knowledge lives here as a **note** — whether it's a daily journal, meeting notes, a book review, or research findings.

### How It Works

- **Notes** are the core unit. Each note has a `type` that hints at its purpose, but you're free to structure content however you like.
- **Tags** help you organize and filter. Assign as many as you need.
- **Links** connect notes together, building a web of knowledge over time.
- **Markdown** is the language — use headings, lists, code blocks, quotes, and more.

### Note Types

| Type | Best for |
|------|----------|
| daily | Journal entries, daily priorities, reflections |
| meeting | Meeting notes, agendas, action items |
| project | Project plans, goals, task tracking |
| book | Book reviews, key takeaways, quotes |
| article | Article summaries, commentary |
| research | Research findings, paper notes, citations |
| reference | How-tos, cheat sheets, reference material |
| general | Everything else |

### Tips

- Use the `summary` field for a quick TL;DR visible in card view
- Pin important notes so they stay at the top
- Link related notes to build your knowledge graph
summary: An introduction to your knowledge base and how to use different note types.
